ety: khod˚, prob. = Prk. खोद(इ) or खोदे(इ) = S. क्षोदय(ति), caus. of rt. क्षुद्
def: v.t. To dig, delve, excavate; to undermine; to scoop, hollow; to engrave; to carve; — to search for; to inquire closely into, to investigate
more: v. a. S. खन् to dig. 1. To dig; delve; excavate; scoop. ' Chanḍī, ghar līpegī?' 'Nahīṅ, nigoṛe, khodūṅgī.' 'Chanḍī, ghar khodegī?' 'Nahīṅ, nigoṛe, līpūngī .' Wom. Prov. 'You shrew, will you plaster the floor?' 'No, you wretch! I'll dig it up.' 'You shrew, will you dig the floor?' 'No, you wretch! I'll plaster it.' 2. To engrave; carve. 3. To investigate ( tahqīqāt k .). khod-kar yā khod khod-ke pūchhnā , v. a. To question or investigate closely; cross-question; prove; fathom. khod-gar, chod-gar , n. m. Slang. A father.
v. a. To dig, delve, hollow, engrave, carve, undermine. 2. To search for.
subs: v.t. (intens.) To dig up; to scoop out, &c.
wety: Derived from Prakrit *𑀔𑁆ō𑀤𑁆𑀤𑁆 (*khōdd). Cognate with Punjabi ਖੋਦਣਾ (khodṇā), Gujarati ખોદવું (khodvũ), Marathi 𑘏𑘻𑘟𑘜𑘹𑘽 (khodṇẽ), Nepali खोदनु (khodanu), Bengali খোদা (khōda).

n. m. S. कुणिका 1. A peg; a tent-peg. Idhar khūṅṭā, udhar khūṅṭā, gāë markhanī, dūdh mīthā . Riddle. Ans. Singhāṛā . On this side a thorn, on that a thorn, a butting cow, sweet milk she gives. 2. The upright post in a sugar mill. khūṅṭā sā baiṭh jānā, yā gaṛ jānā , v. n. To be fixed like a post. khūṅṭe par mārnā , v. a. To spurn; scorn. khūṅṭ'e se baṅdhnā , v. n. 1. To be tied to a post. 2. To be wedded to. qasāī ke khūṅṭe se bāṅdhnā , v. a. 1. To put one in a fix; to confound; expose one to danger. 2. To marry a girl to a cruel person. khūṅṭe ke bal kūdnā , v. n. lit to skip on one's tether. To presume on another's protection.

def: s.f. = khoṭāʼī = khuṭāʼī, q.v. Baseness (of coin), adulteration, alloy; flaw; corruption; deceit, fraud, falseness, &c. &c. (see khoṭā, and khuṭāʼī).

ety: i.q. khoṅdhā = kho or khop, q.v. +S. धा
def: s.m. A (bird's) nest.
s.m.f. Wasteful, extravagant, prodigal; — a spendthrift, a squanderer.
s.f. A hole, cave; a corner, a chasm, gap, fissure, rent; — a long stitch
more: n. f. S. गुहा 1. A rent; fissure. 2. A long stitch. khop bharnā , v. a. To stitch; baste.
s. f. 1. (Sans. गुहा ) A cave, a corner. 2. A rent, a fissure. (Beng.).
subs: (-kī), To sew with long stitches, to baste.
wety: From Sanskrit गुहा (guhā).

def: s.m. = khoṅpā, q.v.
s.m. Cocoa-nut kernel, milk of a cocoa-nut hardened into a ball; — skull; brain (in this sense khoprā).
s.f. The skull, pate, sconce; the brain-pan; a shell
more: n. f. S. खर्पर Pālī, kapparo , 1. The skull; pate. 2. The brainpan ( kapāl , 1). Bhāī! kyā beḍhab khoprī hai! khoprī kā hāḍ , ( Anat .) The bones of the skull. khoprī khā jānā , v. a. lit . to devour one's brains. 1. To eat one's head off. 2. To worry; plague ( bhejā khānā ). 3. To spend or lavish another's property. khoprī ganjī k. , v. a. To cudgel; brain. khoprī meṅ sūrākh k . G. G. To trepan. baiṅḍī khoprī , An ill-disposed or rude man. Yeh baiṅḍī khoprī hai, is se bachte rahnā!
s. f. The skull, a shell, pate. کهوپري کهاجانا khoprī khā-jānā, v. a. To spend or lavish anotherʼs property.
subs: (-kī), 'To devour (one's) skull or brains'; to eat (one's) head off; to torment, worry, plague; to spend or lavish the property (of another)
(-kī), To beat (one's) skull bare or bald; to beat or cudgel severely
(-kī), To make a hole in the skull (of); to trepan.

def: s.f. The skull, pate, sconce; the brain-pan; a shell
subs: (-kī), 'To devour (one's) skull or brains'; to eat (one's) head off; to torment, worry, plague; to spend or lavish the property (of another)
(-kī), To beat (one's) skull bare or bald; to beat or cudgel severely
(-kī), To make a hole in the skull (of); to trepan.

def: s.m. Cocoa-nut kernel, milk of a cocoa-nut hardened into a ball; — skull; brain (in this sense khoprā).
more: n. m. खर्पर 1. ( khopā ) The kernel of a cocoa-nut. Girī, nārial, khopre chaṛheṅ bhavan meṅ ān, 'Jai jai' boleṅ jātrī, Panḍat kareṅ bakhān . Hymn. The pilgrims gifts of cocoa-nuts lay upon the altar, ' Jaë jaë! ' they shout, the while the priests intone their pratter. 2. ( Slang ) Head; skull. Khoprā phoṛ dūṅgā!
s. m. A cocoanut, rather the kernel of a cocoa-nut, or dried half-kernel, kernel.

def: s.f. = khoṛ, q.v.
more: n. f. S. खाद् to eat. An earthen trough from which cattle eat.
n. f. 1. A covering; a lid. 2. ( Rus .) A quilt. 3. A fine skin over the viscera; the guts of cows or sheep, well cleaned and dressed, given to lying-in-women to restore their milk.
n. f. 1. H. khoṭ defect. The wrath of a good spirit. Inke hāṅ pitroṅ kī khoṛ hai . 2. An unlucky moment. 3. Disease.
n. f. H. khaṛag a sword. The crescent made on the forehead with sandal-wood, saffron, etc. by the worshippers of Shiv.
s. f. 1. An alley. 2. A covering. 3. The rage or curse of a god.

ety: S. कुहरं; but cf. khol, and khoḍar
def: s.f. A cavern, cave, &c. ( = khorā, khoh); a small valley; an alley, a lane; — a covering, cover; case ( = khol); a fine skin over the viscera; — the guts of cows or sheep (well cleaned and dressed, given to lying-in women to restore their milk when deficient or lost).
s.f. The wrath or curse of a god or spirit; — an unlucky moment (cf. khoṭ); disease; — an earthen trough (for cattle to eat out of; — cf. khoḍar, and khol).
more: s. m. (dakh.) 1. Defect, fault, blemish: see کلنک kalank, or کهوٿ khoṭ. 2. A large piece of sandal wood.

def: s.m.f. A dialec. form of khoṭ, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) A piece (of wood, esp. of sandal-wood, or of perfume); a block, a log.
adj. (f. -ī), Limping, lame.
s.m. Stocks (for criminals); handcuffs, manacles; — a log of wood (used to encumber an animal whilst grazing), a clog, fetter.
s.m. A pit; cavern, cave, den ( = kho).
more: s. m. (dakh.) Cavern, pit: see غار g̠ẖār.

def: n. prop. Name of one of the warriours of Kai Ḵẖusrau son of Siyāwush.
s.m. = khoḍar, q.v.; — adj. & s.m. = khodarā, or khudrā, q.v. adj. Pitted; rough, uneven, &c. ( = khudrā); — s.m. A kind of pace (in a horse), a trot, an amble (see khudrānā).
s.f. = khor, and khorā, qq.v.
s.f. = khauṛ, q.v. s.f. The tilak or (crescent-shaped) mark made on the forehead (of Hindūs, worshippers of Śiva) with sandal-wood, saffron, &c.

ety: dim. cf khor or khorā, qq.v.
def: s.f. A narrow valley; — an alley; — a covering; cover.
s.f. Wickedness, vice, corruptness, &c.
adj. & s.m. corr. of koṛhī, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Afflicted with leprosy, leprous; — a leper
subs: s.m. One who is stricken with leprosy; a great or grievous sinner.

ety: khoṛ = khoṛ + Prk. इल्लो = S. इलः
def: adj. (Dakh.) Worthy of blame, disgraceful, &c. (see khoṭā).

n. m. S. क्रीडा a play. A marriage ceremony. After the departure of the barāt for the bride's house the bridegroom's female relations act a play in which the younger married sister of the bridegroom is represented as a bridegroom, and his younger brother's wife as a bride, all the other women singing and dancing, etc. the whole night in male dresses in imitation of what is being enacted in the bride's house.
n. A withered tree.
s.m. Stock or stump (of a tree); a tree of which the head and branches are broken off; a dry or withered tree.
more: (dakh. i. q. سوکاجهاڙ) A dry shrub or bush.
s. (dakh.) A dry or withered tree.

def: pleasant (ie. emotions or otherwise); delicious, digestible, savoury (ie. food etc.); charming
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خوشْگُوَار (xōšguwār). By surface analysis, خوش (xoś) + گَوَار (gavār).

def: glad tidings; good news (something positive)
wety: From خوش (xoś, “happy”) + خبر (xabar, “news”) + ی (ī).

def: s.m. (dialec.) Rind, peel, skin, shell; — name of a marauding tribe on the borders of Sind.
adj. Having little or no beard (a man).
more: (Pers. کوسه) adj. Having little or no beard (a man).
s. m. Rind, peel, skin, shell. (Beng.).

def: alternative form of خوش گَوَار (xoś gavār)
s.m. An ear of corn; a spike; a bunch, cluster (of grapes, or dates, &c.; syn. guććhā); — an ear-ring
more: n. m. 1. An ear of corn; a spike. 2. A bunch; cluster ( guchchhā ). khoshah-chīṅ , n. 1. A gleaner. 2. A recipient of a benefaction. khoshah-chīnī , n. f. Gleaning.
s. m. 1. An ear of corn. 2. A bunch (of grapes, &c.). 3. An earing, a spike. خوشه چين ḵẖosha-chīn, A gleaner.
subs: s.m. A gleaner; (fig.) recipient of a benefaction

def: direct plural of خوشی (xośī)
oblique plural of خوشی (xośī)
v.t. To pull out (hairs, &c.); to pluck; to take or snatch away, to dart at and carry off; — to force (into, -meṅ), thrust (into), stick; to tuck (in); to insert; to stuff, cram ( = khoṅsnā).
more: v. a. 1. To pluck (چهيننا). 2. To insert (ارَسنا 1). Koī shālā khosh gayā hogā . Some rascal stuck it in my belt. (The excuse made by a Bengali who was taken armed by the enemy).
v. a. 1. To take or snatch away. 2. To pull out hairs.

def: s.f. & m. Falsehood, deceit, fraud; unfaithfulness, insincerity; vice, evil, wickedness; evil propensity or disposition; fault, defect, blemish, flaw; blunder, mistake, false step; — adulteration, alloy; — loss (in trade or dealing); injury, hurt, harm, mischief
more: (Arab. خطا) s. f. A blunder, vice, blemish, defect, fault. ¶ khūṭ, s. (dakh.) Side: see جانب jānib.
subs: To make a mistake, to blunder; to commit a fault; to practise fraud or deceit, &c.; — to do harm (to, -kā or -kī), to injure, cause loss (to)
(-meṅ), To mix base metal (with), to adulterate
(-meṅ), To detect faults (in); to find fault (with).

def: n. m. कूट See اوگن 1—4, 6, and خطا 1. An alloy. khoṭ k. , v. a. 1. See khatā k . 2. To do harm to. Maiṅ ne tumhārā kyā khoṭ kīyā hai? khoṭ milānā , v. a. To mix with a base metal. khoṭ nikālnā , v. a. To find or pick faults.
s.m. = khoṅtā, q.v. s.m. A (bird's) nest.
more: adj. 1. See برا 7, 8, 15, 17. 2. Defective; faulty. 3. False; spurious; base. 4. Vicious; corrupt; malignant. Jitnā chhoṭā utnā hī khoṭā . Prov. As false as he is small. 5. Dishonest; unfair; fraudulent. 6. Perfidious; treacherous. 7. Malicious; revengeful. khoṭā irādah . A sinister design. khoṭā beṭā , n. m. An undutiful son. khoṭā rāj , n. m. Mal-administration ( burā rāj ). khoṭā sikkā . False coin. khoṭā samā , n. m. See burā samā. khoṭā kharā dekhnā , v. a. To distinguish good from bad. khoṭā muāmilah . Unfair dealing; a dishonest transaction. khoṭī tadbīr . Machination; crooked policy. khoṭī, yā khoṭī kharī sunānā , v. a. To abuse one. khoṭī k. , v. a. 1. To alloy. 2. To mar; spoil. 3. To play false; to do mischief. khoṭī kahnā , v. a. To speak against; backbite; abuse. khoṭī kharī , n. f. Abusive language. khoṭī ghaṛī, yā sāat , n. f. See burā waqt. khoṭī hunḍī , n. f. A fraudulen…

n. m. khoṭī , n. f. A wicked person; a firebrand ( burā , 1). Zor nahīṅ, kuchh zulam nahīṅ, hai merī iskī rājī, Bahut huī bad-nām mulak meṅ, khoṭī khoṭī bājī . Quarrels of co-wives. Nor strength, nor tyranny, we simply did agree, I know the country round rings with my infamy. khuṭ'āī , n. f. khoṭā-pan , n. m. 1. See اوگن 1—4. 2. Flaw; alloy. 3. Defect. Aur kī khuṭāī apne āge aī . Prov. The evil you to others do will come at last on you. 4. Spuriousness. 5. Dishonesty. 6. Villainy. 7. Faithlessness; deceit.
ex: donkey nihari (a hoaxical dish stereotypically associated with Lahore)
wety: Borrowed from Punjabi کھوتا (khotā).

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), False, base, counterfeit, spurious, forged; debased, alloyed, adulterated; faulty, defective, deficient; — false, unreal, untrue, insincere; perfidious, treacherous, deceitful, dishonest, fraudulent; base, corrupt; vicious, immoral, wicked, undutiful evil, bad, sinister; malignant, malicious, spiteful; — a false or perfidious man, a traitor; a wicked person, &c.
more: adj. Perfidious, counterfeit, false, faulty, defective.
subs: To distinguish (or separate) good from bad
or khoṭī kharī sunānā (-ko), To abuse, to use bad language to
(-meṅ?), to alloy, adulterate; to vitiate, mar, spoil; to act falsely (to), play (one) false; to do harm or mischief (to)
To speak ill (of), speak against, backbite, abuse.
wety: From Sanskrit कौट (kauṭa).

def: khoṭā-eṅgī, s. f. (dakh.) See کهوٿاٴي khoṭā-ī.
s. f. Perfidy, falseness, faultiness, defectiveness.

ety: khoṭā, q.v.+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं); — and āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: Baseness (of coin), adulteration, alloy; flaw; corruption; deceit, fraud, falseness, &c. &c. (see khoṭā, and khuṭāʼī).

ety: khoṭā, q.v.+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं); — and āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: Baseness (of coin), adulteration, alloy; flaw; corruption; deceit, fraud, falseness, &c. &c. (see khoṭā, and khuṭāʼī).
direct plural; oblique/vocative singular
s.m. = khoṭā-pan, q.v. Baseness (of coin), adulteration, alloy; flaw; corruption; deceit, fraud, falseness, &c. &c. (see khoṭā, and khuṭāʼī).
oblique plural of کھوتا (khotā, “donkey”)
vocative plural of کھوتا (khotā, “donkey”)
s.m.f. Wasteful, extravagant, prodigal; — a spendthrift, a squanderer.

ety: for khoʼā, perf. part. of khonā; and = S. क्षपित+कः
def: part. adj. & s.m. (f. khoʼī), Lost; destroyed; thrown away, &c.; — any thing or person that is lost; that which is thrown away; refuse, dregs.
more: n. m. 1. Curd. 2. Coarse brick dust.

def: s.m. = khoʼā, q.v. s.m. Pounded brick, coarse brickdust (syn. surkhī); mortar, plaster; — milk inspissated by boiling, curds.
s.m. Christ.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Of or relating to Christ or to His religion, Christian; — a Christian.
poetic form of خُون (xūn, “blood”)
( In comp .) Reading; singing.

ety: Pehl. ḵẖvap; Zend hvāpāo, hu+āpa; S. su+apas
def: adj. & adv. Splendid, beautiful, pretty, charming; good; fine; excellent; pleasing, amiable; — very good; very well; well, excellently, &c.
more: adj. Good. See اچها adj. Apnī maslahat har sha/?/khs khūb jāntā hai . Every man is the best judge of his own interest.

adv. Very good. See اچها adv. 1—5, & 7, 8. Khūb dunyā ko āzmā dekhā, Jis ko dekhā so be-vafā dekhā . The world I've tried and known it well, No truth in them who in it dwell. khūb, kyā khūb , intj. 1. See achchhā , 1. intj. 2. Good! very fine! well-done! bravo! khūb sā , adv. Very much.

adj. Good, excellent, well, beautiful, pleasant, amiable. خوبرو ḵẖūb-rū or خوب روي ḵẖūb-rūʼe (plur. خوب رويان) or خوب صورت ḵẖūb-ṣūrat, adj. Beautiful, a beauty. خوبروي ḵẖūb-rū-ī or خوب صورتي ḵẖūb-ṣūrat-ī, s. f. Beauty, handsomeness. (Plur. خوبان ḵẖūbān, The fair, the beautiful).
subs: (compar. of ḵẖūb), adj. More beautiful; finer; better
(superl.), adj. Most beautiful, fairest; best, finest
ḵẖūb-ṣūrat, adj. & s.f. Having a beautiful face, beautiful, handsome; — a beauty
ḵẖūb-ṣūratī, s.f. Comeliness of face or form, beauty.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خُوب (xūb). First attested in c. 1421 as Middle Hindi خوب (xvb /⁠xūb⁠/).

def: n. m. Beauties; lady-love.
s.m.f. pl. (of ḵẖūb), The fair, the beautiful; (as a sing.) Sweetheart, lady-love.
s.f. An apricot; a dried apricot (with its kernel, or that of an almond inserted).
more: n. f. An apricot; a dried apricot.
s. f. An apricot, or a dried apricot with the kernel of an almond inserted.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خوبانی (xūbānī).

def: v.t. = khubnā, q.v. v.t. To stir, to affect; to enter, penetrate, prick, pierce, stick into (cf. ćubhnā); — to adorn.
more: v. a. 1. To affect, to penetrate, to stick into. 2. To adorn.

def: s.f. Beauty; goodness, excellence; merit, virtue; well-being; pleasantness
more: s. f. Beauty, goodness, excellence, merit, pleasantness, wellbeing. بخوبي ba-ḵẖūbī, Well, excellently.

def: s.f. Name of a seed of a cooling quality.
more: s. m. Name of a seed of a cooling quality.

ety: khub˚ = khubh˚ = Prk. खुब्भे(इ) = S. क्षोभय(ति), rt. क्षुभ्
def: v.t. To stir, to affect; to enter, penetrate, prick, pierce, stick into (cf. ćubhnā); — to adorn.
more: v. n. S. क्षुभ To penetrate (چبهنا).
v. a. 1. To affect, to penetrate, to stick into. 2. To adorn.

def: s.m. = ḵẖabās̤at, q.v.
more: s. m. Malice, malignity, perfidy, impurity.

def: (Persons only), adj. Good looking; pretty; beautiful; handsome. khūb-sūrtī, khūb-ruī , n. f. Beauty; comeliness of face or form. khūb-miltī , Precisely applicable.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خُوبصُورَت (xūb-sūrat, “beautiful”), compound of خُوب (xūb, “well, decent”) + صُورَت (sūrat, “appearance”). Equivalent of خُوب (xūb) + صُورَت (sūrat).

def: adj. See s.v. ḵẖūb. adj. & adv. Splendid, beautiful, pretty, charming; good; fine; excellent; pleasing, amiable; — very good; very well; well, excellently, &c.
subs: (compar. of ḵẖūb), adj. More beautiful; finer; better
(superl.), adj. Most beautiful, fairest; best, finest
ḵẖūb-ṣūrat, adj. & s.f. Having a beautiful face, beautiful, handsome; — a beauty
ḵẖūb-ṣūratī, s.f. Comeliness of face or form, beauty.

def: adj. superl. Best, most beautiful.
s.m. = ḵẖawīd, q.v. s.m. (f.?), A green field, a sown field, a field of green wheat or barley; green wheat or barley cut for fodder ( = vulg. ḵẖūd).
more: pron. Self. خود آرا ḵẖụd-ārā, adj. Setting off self, proud, arrogant. خود بخود ḵẖụd-ba-ḵẖụd, Of thine, his, &c. own accord, of itself, voluntarily. خود بدولت ḵẖụd ba-daulat, You, your worship, sir! His majesty, your majesty, your honour, &c. خود بين ḵẖụd-bīn, adj. Self-conceited, proud, arrogant, presumptuous. خود بيني ḵẖụd-bīn-ī, s. f. Pride, self-conceit. خود پرست ḵẖụd-parast, adj. Self-conceited. خود پرستي ḵẖụd-parast-ī, s. f. Self-sufficiency, self-conceit. خود پسند ḵẖụd-pasand, adj. Self-complacent, self-conceited. خود پسندي ḵẖụd-pasand-ī, s. f. Self-complacency, self-conceit. خود ثنا ḵẖụd-s̤anā, An egotist. خود راٴي ḵẖụd-rāʼī, s. f. Self-conceit. ¶ ḵẖụd-rāʼe, adj. Self-conceited. خود رخصتي ḵẖụd-ruḵẖṣat-ī, Leaving a service of oneʼs own account, in which case it is customary to deduct something from wages. خود رسته ḵẖụd-rusta, or خود رو ḵẖụd-ro, Growing of itself without being sown,…
ex: میں یہ کام خود کروں گا۔
I will do this work myself.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خْوَد (xwad)/خْوُد (xwud).

def: s.f. Dregs, refuse.
selfish, self-centered
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خود غرض.

def: s.f. corr. of خويد ḵẖawīd, q.v. s.m. (f.?), A green field, a sown field, a field of green wheat or barley; green wheat or barley cut for fodder ( = vulg. ḵẖūd).

ety: old P. qadhāi, qudhāi; Pehl. khotāi; Zend qadhāta, qa+dāta, rt. dā = S. sva+dhita, rt. dhā
def: adj. God-worshipping, godly, devout
more: n. m. The self-existent; the self-created; God. 1. Khudā ko yād karo! Remember God. 2. Khudā hāzir o nāzir hai . Prov. God is omnipresent and omniscient. 3. Hamārā bhī Khudā hai . God is with me too. 4. Khudā par bharosā rakkho . Trust in God. 5. Yeh to maiṅ Khudā se chāhtā hūṅ . This is what I pray for. 6. Khudā kā dīyā sir par . Riddle. Chāṅd . Moon. 1. God's lamp overhead. 2. What God sends must be endured. 7. Khudā do sīṅg de, to woh bhī sahe jāte haiṅ . Prov. If God were to give me two horns, even these would be endured. (What cannot be cured must be endured). 8. Khudā dekhā nahīṅ, aql se to pahchānye . Prov. You have not seen God, but you may comprehend him with your understanding. 9. Khudā ko hāzir o nāzir jān-kar . Remembering that God is all present and all seeing. (The form of oath used in courts). 10. Khudā se ḍaro! Have the fear of God at least! (Said to one who is lying). 11…

s. m. God. خدا پرست ḵẖudāparast, adj. God-worshipper, godly. خداپرستي ḵẖudā-parast-ī, s. f. The worship of God. خدا ترس ḵẖudā-tars (ترس part. act. of ترسيدن To fear) adj. 1. Fearing God. 2. Virtuous. خدا خدا کرکي ḵẖudā ḵẖudā karke, With difficulty, by hook or by crook. خدا حافظ ḵẖudā ḥāfiz̤, God (is) the protector; adieu, farewell. خدا داني ḵẖudā-dān-ī, s. f. The knowledge of God. خدا شناس ḵẖudā-shinās, adj. Knowing God. خداشناسي ḵẖudā-shinās-ī, s. f. Knowledge of God. خدا طلبي ḵẖudā-t̤alab-ī, s. f. n. f. An axiom. khoṭī, yā burī bāt , n. f. Bad or foul language; strong language; abuse. gol bāt, lapeṭwāṅ, du-bhedli, yā du-bheslī bāt , n. f. A vague or ambiguous expression; an equivocal or ambiguous speech; an entangled or doubtful matter. ghar-kī bāt , A domestic affair; an easy matter; a friendly transaction. Hamārī tumhārī to ghar kī bāt hai. gahrī bāt , n. f. A deep thought; a grave or difficult matter. laṭh-mār bāt, kaṛī, yā sakht bāt , n. f. Harsh words; sharp or crooked answers.
s.m. Galangal, Alpinia galanga.

def: adv. Open; uncovered; — openly, publicly; freely, without restraint; at freedom or leisure; at ease (e.g. khulke baiṭhnā); — copiously; to (one's) satisfaction.

more: v. a. To indulge openly in vices which were formerly practised in secret.

ety: khul˚ = Prk. खुड्ड(इ) or खुल्ल(इ) = S. खुड्य(ते), rt. खुड्
def: v.n. To open, come open or undone; to open, expand, blow (as a flower; com. khilnā); to open out, unravel; to be opened (as a knot, or a road for traffic, &c.); to be disentangled, be unravelled; to be untied or unfastened; to be uncovered, be unfolded, be exposed, be laid bare; to be laid or cut open, be dissected, be analyzed; — to be expanded, be widened or enlarged; to be developed; — to be solved (as a problem or mystery), to be made known, be disclosed, be divulged, be revealed (to, -par); — to become clear, evident, or intelligible (to, -par); — to get loose; to be let loose, be set free (from restraint or control); to be loosed (as the tongue); to be dispersed (as clouds), to become clear, to clear up (the sky); to be dissipated or lost (as money at play); to be dissolved (as the marriage-bond); to be removed (as an obstacle or check); to be cleared (as a drain); to be broken (as meditation); to be laid aside (as restraint); — to begin, commence (as work); to start, leave (as a coach, or a railway train); to move (in chess); — to be opened, be started, be set up, be established (a shop, or a business, &c.); to be restored, be renewed (as withheld pay, &c.); to be recovered, to come back (as appetite); — to expand, dilate, or open kindly (upon); to be intimate or familiar (with, -par); to become favourable (the fates, or destiny); — to acquire fulness, clearness, brightness, or depth (a colour); to stand out well or conspicuously, to appear to advantage (with or on, -par), to go or match well (with, as one colour with another, &c.)
more: v. n. 1. To be opened (a knot); to be solved. Moamma khul gayā . 2. To be opened out (stitches). Sīvan khul gaī . 3. To be disentangled, unravelled. 4. To be untied, unfastened, unbuttoned, unfolded. 5. To be let loose; to get loose. Ghoṛā khul gayā . 6. To be set free, removed (from restraint or control). Rok khul gaī . 7. To be removed (obstacles), cleared, cleaned. Morī khulnā . 8. To be lost (in gambling). Sau rupaë khul gaë . 9. To open; to be opened, dissected, analyzed, laid bare. 10. To be cut open (the head). 11. ( Dehlī ) To be dissolved (matrimonial bonds). Ek amīr kā laṛkā kkulā hai . Wom. 12. To move (in chess). Yeh mohrā abhī nahīṅ khulā . 13. To be cut or opened (a passage, channel, line). Saṛak khulī . 14. To be expanded, widened, enlarged. 15. To be disclosed, revealed; to get wind. 16. To be exposed, betrayed. Bāt us par jo na thī ab tak khulī so khul gaī, Bazm meṅ uskī…

(see کهول khol ) v. n. 1. To open, be revealed, be expanded, be untied or unfolded, be disclosed or uncovered, be let loose or be loose, be laid aside (restraint), be dispersed (clouds or rain), to clear up (the sky or rain), to be broken (meditation, &c.).
v. n. (dakh.) To bloom or open (a flower). ¶ khilnā, v. n. To bloom.
subs: v.n. intens. of khulnā (and the more common form).
wety: From Bengali খুলনা (khulna).

def: v.n. To be inflamed with anger; to be angry, vexed, or displeased (cf. jhulasnā).

ety: inf. n. of خلص 'to be pure'
def: s.m. Purity; sincerity, candour, integrity, friendship, affection.

ety: v.n. fr. خمر 'to cover'; see ḵẖamr, 'wine'
def: s.m. Intoxicating, intoxication; the effects of intoxication, pain and headache, &c. occasioned by drinking, crapulence, crop-sickness; headache or sickness (arising from want of sleep, &c.); languor; languishing appearance of the eyes (the effect of drinking, or of drowsiness, or of love, &c.); languishing look
more: n. m. خمر wine. The reaction, or after effects of drinking; hot coppers; drowsiness; langour; a languishing look; drooping eyes. 1. Nashā us ne pīyā, khumār tumheṅ chaṛhā? Prov. He drank the wine, why have you a head on? (Said to a great man's relatives or connections who give themselves airs). 2. Yak nigāh meṅ kīyā hai mast mujhe, Iskī āṅkhoṅ meṅ kyā khumārī hai . Walī. One glance shot from her eye intoxicate I grew, Such juice seductive doth her wanton eyes bedew! 3. Sickness (from drink, wakefulness, or headache). khumār-ālūdah , adj. 1. Drunk; intoxicated. 2. Languishing, languid, drooping (eyes).

(from خمر) s. m. 1. Sickness, headach, &c. the effect of drinking, crapulence. 2. Intoxicating. 3. Languishing appearances of the eyes, the effect of love, of drowsiness, of drinking, &c. خمارآلود ḵẖumār-ālūd or خمارآلوده ḵẖumār-ālūda, adj. Intoxicating (eyes), intoxicated. خمارخانه ḵẖumār-ḵẖāna, s. m. A tavern. ¶ ḵẖammār, adj. Drunkard, much given to drink, cropsick.
subs: or ḵẖumār-ālūda, adj. Intoxicated; intoxicating (eyes); languishing (eyes)
s.f. The alleviating the effects of intoxication; anything used for that purpose, as tea, soda-water, &c.).

ety: Pehl. khun; Zend vohuni; S. vasā
def: s.m. Blood; killing, slaughter, murder
more: n. m. 1. Blood; gore. 2. Slaughter; murder; homicide. khūn-ālūdah . Stained with blood; bloody; reeking with blood. khūn āṅkhoṅ meṅ utarnā , v. n. To be bloodshot (the eyes) with rage. khūn bigaṛnā , v. n. 1. To become impure (the blood). 2. To be leprous. khūṅ-bahā , n. m. The price of blood; blood money. khūn pīnā , v. a. To drink one's blood. Merā roz khūn tū pītā, Tujhe āj nahīṅ chhoṛūṅ jītā . (To a bug). You drink my blood from day to day, Alive you shall not 'scape today. khūn-i-jigar pīnā yā khānā , v. n. 1. To restrain one's emotions, anger, grief, etc. 2. To work very hard; to work one's self to death. khūn chalnā yā nikalnā , v. a. To bleed or cause blood to flow. /?/khūn /?/kharābā, /?/khūn /?/khachchar , n. m. Bloodshed; bloody work. khūṅ-khuār ; Pop. khūṅ-khār , n. m. 1. Blood-sucker; an animal that preys on others. khūṅ-khuār , adj. Blood-thirsty; sanguinary; murderous; cru…

s. m. 1. Blood. 2. Murder, killing. خوناب ḵẖūn-āb or خونابه ḵẖūn-āba, s. m. Bloody water, tears of blood: see, also, خون نابه ḵẖūn-nāba. خون آشام ḵẖūn-āshām, adj. Drinking blood; ferocious, cruel. خون افشاني ḵẖūn-afshān-ī, s. f. Pouring out of blood, shedding of blood. خون آلوده ḵẖūn-ālūda, adj. Stained with blood, bloody. خون بار ḵẖūn-bār, Raining or weeping blood (generally spoken of the eyes of a lover). خونباري ḵẖūn-bār-ī, s. f. The raining or weeping of blood. خون بستگي ḵẖūn-basta-gī, s. f. Clotting or binding together with blood. خون بسته ḵẖūn-basta, Bound or clotted together with blood. جون بـﮩ‍ا ḵẖūn-bahā, s. m. The price of blood, forfeit or retaliation for murder. خون جگرپينا يا کهانا ḵẖūn-i-jigar-pīnā or -khānā, To work oneʼs self to death. خون چاٿنا ḵẖūn chāṭnā, To be stained with blood (a weapon). خونچکان ḵẖūn-chakān, adj. Dropping blood. خون خرابه ḵẖūn-ḵẖarāba, s. m. Bloody…
subs: ḵẖūn-āba, s.m. Bloody water; blood; a torrent of blood; tears of blood; blood turned to water (through grief)
s.f. Pouring out of blood, shedding of blood
(āṅkhoṅ-meṅ) utarnā, To be blood-shot (the eyes, — with rage, &c.)
adj. Shedding (or raining) blood (generally spoken of the eyes of a lover)

The blood to become impure; to be leprous

s.m. The price of blood, blood-money, a fine for blood-shedding
v.n. To pass blood (as from hemorrhoids, &c.)
To suppress (one's) feelings, restrain (one's) emotion, or anger, or grief, &c.; — to consume (one's own) life-blood; to vex or worry (oneself) to death; — to work (oneself) to death
v.n. Blood to issue, to bleed
s.m. Bloodshed, bloody work, deeds of death; mutual slaughter
To do deeds of death, to make great havoc, &c.
adj. & s.m. Bloodthirsty, murderous, sanguinary, cruel; — a bloodthirsty man (or other animal), a murderer; an animal that preys on others
s.f. Bloodthirstiness; blood-shedding, slaughter, murder; — sorrow
adj. & s.m. Blood-shedding, murderous; — a blood-shedder, executioner, murderer, cut-throat, assassin
(-kā), To be distracted in consequence of having assassinated a person; — to be bent on taking the life or lives (of)
'To turn the blood white'; (fig.) to be unkind; to be displeased
To lack natural affection; to be unkind
adj. Thirsting for the blood (of, -kā), desirous of (one's) death
s.m. Compensation or fine for bloodshed, blood-wite; retaliation for blood-shedding, blood for blood
(-kā), To shed the blood (of), to kill, murder, assassinate; — to lavish, squander, waste
adj. 'Whose blood is taken,' doomed to be slain or to die, at the point of death
v.n. A murder to be committed; to be murdered; — to be wasted, be squandered.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خُون (xūn). First attested in c. 1564 as Middle Hindi خون (xvn /⁠xūn⁠/).

more: v. n. 1. To dislike; hate. 2. To be cross, waspish; to turn on one. Gaī hiye kī phūṭ āg ko ghar ghar ḍole, Jā se māṅge āg, nār vohī khunsātī bole . Song. Smokers. Gone is his shame, for fire from house to house he goes, Each one of whom he begs on him hard words bestows.

n. f. Coughing. 1. Khur khāṅsī, terī dāī ke gale meṅ phāṅsī! If baby coughs hang the nurse. 2. Khur khāṅsī, baniye ke jāë! Baniye ke ghar ghiu, guṛ khāë . Nursery rhymes. Go, nasty cough! to the grocer's speed! On sugar and butter there you'll feed. khur khur k. , v. n. To cough.

adj. (in comp.) Possessed of a hoof, hoofed; — s.m. The heel (of a shoe or sandal; — cf. khurī).
s. Dirty water: a floodgate.

ety: prob. fr. S. क्षुरे+तक्ष; cf. kharoćnā, kharoṭnā, khureṅćnā
def: v.t. To scrape; to scratch; to scratch out, to erase
